steps.title: Recognize the slope-intercept form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = -9x + 9$ is in the form $y = mx + b$, where the constant term $b$ equals the y-coordinate of the y-intercept.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: From $f(x) = -9x + 9$, we identify $b = 9$. We can verify this by evaluating at $x = 0$: $f(0) = -9(0) + 9 = 9$. The y-coordinate of the y-intercept is \$9$.
